steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure of the blank
steps.content:
The sentence reads: "Yerevan is the capital city of Armenia and, ______ roughly one million people, home to an impressive 36 percent of Armenia's total population."

The blank sits between two commas, forming an offset descriptive phrase (an absolute or participial modifier). This structure requires a present participle — a verb form ending in -ing — not a finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘contained’: A past participle creates a passive meaning ("being contained") that does not fit the sense of the city holding a population. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘contains’: A finite present-tense verb cannot function as an offset modifier between commas; this produces a grammatically broken sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘containing’: The present participle correctly fills the offset modifier slot, describing Yerevan as the city that contains roughly one million people. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘has contained’: A finite perfect-tense verb cannot function as an offset modifier; this also breaks the sentence structure. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
